channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: SoftBank is creating a robotics company that builds data centers — and already eyeing a $100B IPO
item.description: You need infrastructure to build AI a and robots, but apparently you also need AI and robots to build infrastructure.

item.title: The Ownership Framework: How to Use AI to Write Code Without Losing Your Mind
item.description: The Ownership Framework: How to Use AI to Write Code Without Losing Your Mind Right now, across thousands of dev teams, the same scene is playing out. A developer opens Cursor or Copilot,...

item.title: I Looked at Developer Blogs and Platforms That Accept Guest Posts — Here’s Where I’d Pitch to Reach Software Developers
item.description: When you’re trying to reach developers through content, publishing on your own blog is only one option. The other route is guest posting. That means finding developer blogs and platforms that...

item.title: I built a Chrome Extension to validate sitemap.xml in one click — here's why and how
item.description: The itch I had to scratch Every time I ran a technical SEO audit, I'd end up doing the same manual dance: copy the sitemap URL, paste it into an online validator, wait, get back a wall of output,...

item.title: The AI Hype vs. Architecture Reality: Why Big Tech is Locking Down Models in 2026
item.description: The gap between Silicon Valley's public AI narratives and the actual technical reality we face as developers has never been wider. If you are building AI-integrated applications right now, you are...

item.title: Biology is a Burrito: A text- and visual-based journey through a living cell
item.description: Article URL: https://burrito.bio/essays/biology-is-a-burrito Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47957714 Points: 15 # Comments: 2

item.title: Google Cloud Next '26 Made One Thing Clear: Agents Need Infrastructure, Not Hype
item.description: This is a submission for the Google Cloud NEXT Writing Challenge Google Cloud Next '26 had a very loud headline: the agentic enterprise is here. But the more interesting story, at least for...

item.title: Defending Your Code: Surviving the 2026 Node and Python Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Running a simple package installation command in your terminal used to be a mundane task. Today, it feels more like playing a high stakes game of Russian roulette. The open source ecosystem is...

item.title: StocksPI: An AI-Based Platform for Beginner Stock Market Investors
item.description: Introduction The stock market plays a crucial role in financial growth and wealth creation. However, beginner investors often hesitate to participate due to lack of knowledge, fear of financial...

item.title: Where the Goblins Came From
item.description: Article URL: https://openai.com/index/where-the-goblins-came-from/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47957688 Points: 86 # Comments: 22

item.title: " SaaS in 2026 Is the Plumbing, Not the Hype "
item.description: Why I'm Building SaaS in 2026 SaaS as reliable plumbing for fragile agents arunkant arunkant arunkant Follow Apr 29 Why I'm Building SaaS in 2026 #saas #ai #agents #workflows 10 reactions Comments...

item.title: Amazon’s cloud business is surging — and so is its capital spending
item.description: The e-commerce giant is making more money than expected from AWS but it's also spending a lot, and will continue to do so in the near term, its chief executive said.

item.title: Sources: Anthropic could raise a new $50B round at a valuation of $900B
item.description: The maker of Claude has received multiple pre-emptive offers at valuations in the $850 billion to $900 billion range, according to sources familiar with the matter.

item.title: Elon Musk’s worst enemy in court is Elon Musk
item.description: About five hours into Elon Musk's testimony, I typed the following sentence into my notes: "I have never been more sympathetic to Sam Altman in my life." Musk's direct testimony was an improvement...

item.title: On the stand, Elon Musk can’t escape his own tweets
item.description: Elon Musk took the stand for the second day for his attempt to legally dismantle OpenAI.

item.title: Meta is still burning money on AR/VR
item.description: Meta is losing billions on Reality Labs each quarter, and its AI expenditures are only going to increase its spending.

item.title: Satya Nadella says he’s ready to ‘exploit’ the new OpenAI deal
item.description: Microsoft gets to offer OpenAI's tech to its cloud customers and doesn't have to pay for it. "We fully plan to exploit it," Nadella said.

item.title: How Elon Musk Squeezed OpenAI: They 'Are Gonna Want to Kill Me’
item.description: Tensions flared on the third day of trial in Musk v. Altman as OpenAI’s lawyers cross-examined Musk.

item.title: Microsoft says it has over 20M paid Copilot users, and they really are using it
item.description: Despite the lingering perception that no one really uses Copilot, Microsoft said on Wednesday that the number of users and engagement is growing.

item.title: Google Cloud surpasses $20B, but says growth was capacity-constrained
item.description: Google Cloud topped $20B in quarterly revenue for the first time, fueled by surging demand for AI. But capacity constraints mean it could have grown even faster.

item.title: Grindr — yes, Grindr — won the WHCD party circuit
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge subscribers about technology, politics, and technology learning how to politick. If you're not a subscriber but would like to support our...

item.title: Splatoon Raiders preorders for the Switch 2 are nearly 20 percent off
item.description: Nintendo recently announced a new pricing policy, which knocks $10 off the cost of digital versions of future first-party titles exclusive to the Nintendo Switch 2. Splatoon Raiders, for instance,...

item.title: Google gains 25M subscriptions in Q1, driven by YouTube and Google One
item.description: Google added 25M paid subscriptions in Q1, reaching 350M total, as YouTube and Google One grow.

item.title: Microsoft reports sinking Xbox revenue as its cloud business climbs
item.description: Microsoft's Xbox hardware revenue continues to tumble, with the company revealing a 33 percent decline as part of its earnings report released on Wednesday. Even though the rest of Microsoft's...

item.title: Google Search queries hit an ‘all time high’ last quarter
item.description: Google Search queries hit an "all time high" in the first quarter of 2026, according to a statement from CEO Sundar Pichai published as part of Alphabet's earnings on Wednesday. "Our AI...

item.title: All the evidence unveiled so far in Musk v. Altman
item.description: The Musk v. Altman trial is underway, and that means exhibits, or the evidence to be presented in court, are being revealed piece by piece. So far, email exchanges, photos, and corporate documents...

item.title: Ubuntu’s AI plans have Linux users looking for a ‘kill switch’
item.description: Canonical's plan to add AI features to Ubuntu has some users asking for "a version of Ubuntu that does not include these features," while others say they'll stick with older versions of the Linux...

item.title: SAP-Related npm Packages Compromised in Credential-Stealing Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are sounding the alarm about a new supply chain attack campaign targeting SAP-related npm Packages with credential-stealing malware. According to reports from Aikido...

item.title: Motorola just revealed the Razr Fold’s price and hoo boy
item.description: 2026 is shaping up to be a tough year to launch a high-end phone. The memory crisis has phone prices rising across the board, so an already expensive phone risks becoming a much too expensive...

item.title: The new Razr Ultra is still the best-looking phone out there
item.description: Look, you're not going to find much new on the 2026 version of the Motorola Razr Ultra. There's a new main camera sensor, a slightly bigger battery, and a higher price: $1,499, up from $1,299. But...
